more: adj. Divisible; liable to partition; according to allotment.

ety: inf. n. ii of قصر 'to shorten'
def: s.f. Defect, failure, omission, shortcoming; mistake, error, fault, offence, crime misdemeanour; guilt, blame
more: n. f. قصر diminishing. 1. Omission; failure; neglect. 2. A mistake; error; fault. 3. Offence; misdemeanor; crime; guilt. taqsīr-vār, taqsīr-mand , adj. 1. Blameable; faulty. 2. Guilty; culpable; criminal. be-taqsīr ; adj. Blameless; innocent.
(inf. ii. of قصر or قصور) s. f. Fault, errour, crime, offence. (Plur. تقصيرات taḳṣīrāt ). تقصيروار taḳṣīr-wār, Blamable, faulty, criminal, guilty, convict, culpable.
subs: taqṣīr-wār, adj. Blamable, culpable, faulty, criminal, guilty.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَقْصِير (taqṣīr).

ety: inf. n. ii of کشر 'to be much, or abundant,' &c.
def: s.f. Multiplying, increasing, augmenting, enlarging.

ety: inf. n. v of کسر 'to break'
def: s.f. Breaking to pieces, shattering; carrying (figures in arithmetic); dividing so as to produce a fraction.
more: (inf. ii. of کسر) s. f. 1. Breaking to pieces. 2. Carrying (figures, in arithmetick). 3. Dividing so as to produce a fraction.

def: (inf. ii. of کثر) s. f. Augmenting, enlarging, increasing.
s.f. pl. (of taqṣīr), Shortcomings, omissions, errors, failings; offences, crimes.

ety: onomat.
def: s.m. The sound of feet, &c. ( = ṭak-ṭak).
more: n. f. S. तिक to go. lit . Go! go! A call to bullocks to go on. 1. Tik tik karte thak gaë, par ṭaṭṭū na chalā . 2. Āā jāne, tik tik jāne, kahe sune se rahe khaṛā, Kaheṅ Kabīr, suno bhaī sādhoṅ, yā mānas se bail bhalā . Kabīr.

def: s.f. Rapping, rap, &c. = ṭhak-ṭhak, q.v. (cf. tak-tak). adj. See s.v. thak. s.m. A boundary pillar built of masonry (in opp. to ḍhūʼā, 'a mud pillar').
more: s. f. See تک تک tak-tak.

def: s.m. = ṭak-ṭakī, q.v. s.f. A carpenter's level (com. ṭakṭakī).
v.t. = ṭhakṭhakānā, q.v. v.t. To knock, rap, tap, pat, sound (cf. khaṭkhaṭānā).

ety: tak, redupl.+Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A carpenter's level (com. ṭakṭakī).
more: E. n. f. 1. A steadfast look; stare; gaze. 1. Man. ' Nain ṭakāṭak lag rahe, chhuṭī na dil kī bhūk, Bin chābhai ras nā mile, kyā nārī kyā ūkh .' Woman. ' Nainā ṭakāṭak dūr hiṅ bhalā, yā jag māhīṅ ke rīt, Ghan umṛat sobhat bhalo, barsat kādū kīṅch . Tir. 'I gaze and gaze with fixed look, my heart still longs in vain, Unsqueezed no juice you get, be 't woman or a sugarcane.' 'To gaze from far is much the best, the world hath so decreed, The hanging clouds are fair to see, the rains but puddle breed.' 2. Gaë din ṭikṭikī ke bāṅdhne ke, Ab āṅkheṅ rahtī haiṅ do do pahar band . The days of ogling are gone by, For hours these eyes now closed up lie. 2. The triangle; a tripod. Ṭikṭikī se bāṅdh dīyā. ṭukur ṭukur dekhnā , v. a. To stare; gaze; look steadfastly.

ety: inf. n. ii of قطع 'to cut off'
def: s.f. Cutting into parts, dissection; scanning (of verse); the cæsura or pause (in reading poetry)
more: (inf. ii. of قطع) s. f. 1. The cæsura or pause in reading poetry. 2. Scanning of verse. 3. Cutting into parts, dissection.
wety: From Arabic تَقْطِيع (taqṭīʕ), verbal noun of قَطَعَ (qaṭaʕa).

ety: inf. n. ii of قطر 'to drop'
def: s.f. Causing to come away drop by drop; distilling; distillation; passing (urine, &c.) in drops; strangury; diabetes; gonorrhœa.
more: (inf. ii. of قطر) s. f. Distilling, dropping (particles of urine), strangury.

def: n. f. Scanning. taqtī k .; H. chhand-mātrā-ginnā , v. a. To scan.
s.m. = takuʼā, q.v. s.m. A spindle ( = taklā).
more: s. m. A spindle. See تکوا.

ety: S. तर्कु+कः
def: s.m. A spindle ( = taklā).
more: s. m. A spindle. See تکلا taklā.

def: s.m. Fear of God, piety; preserving or guarding oneself from sin; abstinence; continence
more: n. m. قوي Abstinence; chastity; sobriety; piety.
s. m. Piety, abstinence, the fear of God. اهل تقويٰٰ ahl-i-taḳwạ̄, Pious.
(inf. v. of قوي) s. f. Assisting, (particularly, tenants by advances, remitting rents, &c.).
subs: adj. & s.m. Pious, devout; — the pious; a pious man, one who fears God.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْویٰ (taqwā), borrowed from Arabic تَقْوَى (taqwā).

ety: tak˚ = tāk˚ = Prk. तक्क˚ = S. तर्क+wāhī = wāʼī = Prk. आव+अइआ = S. आपि+ता+इका
def: s.f. Looking sharp after, watching, overseeing, superintending.
more: s. f. Watching, overseeing, superintending, looking sharp after (as after a silversmith).

ety: inf. n. ii of قوم 'to be standing or erect'
def: s.f. Making straight or erect; setting in order, ordering, regulating, adjusting, directing aright; fixing, determining, settling; estimating, rating; adjustment, proportion, symmetry; — an almanac, a calendar
more: n. f. قوم making straight. See پترا taqvīm-i-pārīnah , n. m. An old or useless thing.
(inf. ii. of قوم or قيام) s. f. An almanack, ephemeris, calendar.
s. f. Causing to be straight or erect.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْوِیم (taqwīm), from Arabic تَقْوِيم (taqwīm).

ety: inf. n. ii of کون 'to be'
def: s.f. Causing to be, giving a being or existence (to); originating; creating.
more: (inf. ii. of کون) s. f. Causing to be, making to exist.

ety: for A. تقوية, inf. n. ii of قوي
def: s.f. Strengthening, confirming, establishing; strength; corroboration; invigoration; support, aid; assurance, confidence; comfort; trust, reliance
more: n. f. قوي strengthening. Corroboration; support.
(inf. ii. of قوي) s. f. Corroboration, strengthening, establishing, confirming, confidence, strength, assurance, invigoration, support, comfort, reliance, trust, aid.
subs: (-kī), To strengthen, confirm; to sustain, support, back; to inspirit, encourage, &c.

def: s.m. A pillow, bolster, cushion; anything upon which one leans, prop, support; reliance, trust; the reserve of an army; a place of repose; the stand or abode of a faqīr
more: n. m. 1. A pillow; bolster ( gāo-takyā ). Takye ke nīche rakh dīyā . He has put it under his pillow (and forgotten it). 2. Prop; back; support. 3. The abode of a faqīr . 4. A reserve of an army. 5. Reliance; trust. Takyā kīye baiṭhā hūṅ. takye-dār , n. m. A Mah. religious mendicant; a faqīr. Bābā, takye-dār ko bhī koī paisā! Bestow a pice, Sir, on the devotee! takyā denā , E. v. n. lit . To give one his pillow. To receive one with respect. takyā k. yā lagānā , v. n. To lean, rest or rely upon. takyā-kalām, sukhan-takyā , n. m. An expletive; a needless repetition, a habitual or favorite phrase, as 'do you see,' 'do you understand.' gal-takyā , n. m. A pillow for the cheek.

s. m. 1. A pillow, bolster. 2. A faḳīrʼs stand. 3. The reserve of an army. 4. Reliance. تکيه دار takiya-dār, A faḳīr, a dervis. تکيه دينا takiya denā, To receive respectfully. تکيه کرنا takiya karnā, To support, to encourage. تکيه کسي پرکرنا takiya kisī par karnā, To depend upon. تکيه کلام takiya-kalām, or سخن تکيه suḵẖan-takiya, s. m. A cant word introduced into oneʼs conversation without any meaning, an expletive. تکيه لگانا takiya lagānā, To lean, to bolster. تکيه نشين takiya-nishīn, A faḳīr, a dervis.
subs: (-ko), lit. 'To give (one) a pillow to recline against'; to receive with respect; — to lean or rest (upon or against)
(-par), To lean, rest, or recline (upon); to depend or rely (upon)
s.m. An expletive; a needless word or phrase repeatedly or habitually introduced into speech (such as the English 'do you see'; 'do you understand,' &c.)
(-par), To lean (upon or against), &c. = takya karnā (-par)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَکْیَه (takya).

def: a faqīr, a dervish
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَکْیَهدَار (takya-dār).

def: a faqīr, a dervish
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَکْیَهنِشِین (takya-nišīn).

ety: inf. n. ii of کذب 'to lie'
def: s.f. Accusing of falsehood, giving (one) the lie
more: (inf. ii. of کذب) s. f. Accusing of falsehood, giving the lie.

ety: S. तल्लः
def: s.m. Pond, pool, tank, lake ( = tālāb).
more: n. m. S. ताल 1. Beating time (in music); musical time or measure; chime. Tīn tāl par ḍholak bāje, ek tāl par ḍanḍā, Jaun tāl par jogin nāche, wohī tāl par lauṅḍā. Jogīrā . 2. A pair of cymbals. 3. Clapping the hands together. 4. The glass or pebble of an eye glass. tāl ṭhoknā , v. n. See kham ṭhoknā . tāl se be-tāl honā , v. n. To be out of tune. tāl mel , n. m. See اتفاق (1 to 5, 7). tāl mel khānā , v. n. To agree. See ittefāq honā . terah-tālī , n. f. 1. A perfect dancer. 2. An accomplished coquette. 3. A very artful woman.

n. f. 1. A stack (اتَالا 2.). 2. Evasion ( ālā bālā 1.) 3. A bell attached to the necks of cattle. ṭāl mārnā , v. a. To give a twist to the beam in weighing. ṭāl k . v. n. 1. To set up a ṭāl or heap of wood, straw, etc. for sale. 2. To postpone; put off; delay.

s. m. 1. ( ताल ) Beating time (in musick), musical time or measure, chime. 2. Slapping or clapping the hands together or against the arms, &c. 3. A musical instrument of bell-metal or brass (a sort of cymbal) played with a stick. 4. ( ताड ) The palm tree. (Borassus flabelliformis). 5. ( तडाग ) A pond. تال دينا tāl denā, To chime: to slap the hands on the arms before fighting. تال مارنا ياٿهوکنا tāl-mārnā or -ṭhoknā, To strike the hand against the arms preparatory to wrestling.
s. m. Cymbals used by devotees, and frequently an accompaniment to the t̤āʼịfas (Qanoon-e-islam).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ताल (tāla).

ety: S. अअट्टालरोलर?
def: s.f. A station or place for storing wood, grain, grass, and the like, and where it is sold; a store of wood, grain, chaff, &c.; a stack (of wood), heap (of grain, &c.), a rick
more: 1. verbal n. f. (from ٿالنا) Evasion, putting aside, putting off or out of the way, rejecting a request. 2. s. f. A heap (of grain, &c.), a stack, a rick. 3. Baldness from age. 4. A turn or trick in wrestling. ٿال کرنا ṭāl karna, 1. To put off. 2. To heap. ٿال مارنا ṭāl mārnā, 1. To heap. 2. To turn the scale fraudulently in weighing. ٿال مٿال ṭāl maṭāl, Fraud, deceit: see ٿال مٿول.
subs: (-kī), To set up a ṭāl or store of wood, &c.; to make a stack, heap, or rick (of)
(-kī), To heap up, to heap; — to turn the scale fraudulently (in weighing), to give the beam a twist (and thus diminish the true weight).

ety: S. ताली
def: s.f. A small bell (attached to the neck of an ox, or a cow, or an elephant).
Tel Aviv (a city in central Israel); Tel Aviv (a district in central Israel)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَلّ أَبِيب (tall ʔabīb), ultimately from Hebrew תֵּל אָבִיב (tel āvīv).

def: v. n. To get out of the way, to disappear, to vanish, to pass away.

ety: v.n. of ṭālnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Passing over, going beyond (a fixed time); putting off, deferring; evasion, subterfuge, shuffling, prevarication, chicane; putting or turning aside, putting out of the way, deterring; rejecting (a request); — a turn or trick in wrestling; — baldness from age)
subs: ṭāl-maṭāl, ṭālam-ṭol, ṭalmaṭol, s.m. Postponement; disappointment, evasion, &c. = ṭāl
v.n. To practise delay, to put off; to practise evasion or subterfuge; to temporize; to shuffle, prevaricate, &c. (see ṭālnā).

def: adj. 1. Bitter (H. kaṛvā ). 2. Acrid; acrimonious. talkh k . v. a. 1. To embitter; to make bitter. 2. To throw cold water on; to damp. talkh yā kaṛvā mizāj , adj. Ill-tempered.
more: adj. 1. Bitter, acrimonious, acrid, rancid. 2. Malicious, sorrowful. تلخ رو talḵẖ-rū, Morose, surly, stern. تلخ دانه talḵẖ-dāna, Darnel, tares. تلخ کام talḵẖ-kām, adj. Bitter in palate. تلخ کامي talḵẖ-kām-ī, s. f. Bitterness to the palate. تلخ کرنا talḵẖ karnā, To imbitter or make bitter. تلخ گو talḵẖ-go, adj. Plain spoken, harsh. تلخ مزاج talḵẖ-mizāj, adj. Ill-tempered.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تلخ (talx).

ety: Prk. तालओ; S. तालकः
def: s.m. Look, padlock
more: n. m. S. तालक A lock. tālā toṛnā . To break open or force a lock; break open; break into a house. tālā bhiṛnā yā lagnā , v. n. To be locked up (a house), ruined. Bhiṛ jāve Rām tero tālā! Mo pe pīsan kā dukhṛā ḍālā! Wom. Rus. song. So be't your house destroyed—a lock put on outside! To the task of grinding corn each day, O Ram, you have me tied. tālā kunjī , n. m. A boys' game.

n. m. 1. Evasion; putting off; shuffling; equivocation; fencing. 2. Prevarication; tergiversation; chicanery. ṭālam-ṭol k . v. a. 1. To put off ( ālā bālā k . 1.). 2. To dodge; parry; temporize; fence; sham. 3. To evade; elude by stratagem; prevaricate; equivocate.
subs: v.n. To be locked up, to be closed (a house or shop)
(-kā), To break open or to force a lock; to break into (a house)
(-meṅ), To put a padlock (on), to fasten with a padlock ( = qufl lagānā)
wety: From Sanskrit तालक (tālaka).

def: s.f. The leathern fence worn by archers on the left arm.
more: n. m. Cor. of A. tilā gold. The gold lace border of a turban.

n. m. S. तल 1. The bottom; lowest part; keel. 2. The sole of a boot or shoe. 1. Merī jūtī ke tale se! Wom. 2. Baṛe-vāle * se mochan kyoṅ laṛe hai, Tale † se kām donoṅ kā chale hai . Jest. Mazāq . 3. A layer; coating. Talā de-kar hāṅḍī chaṛhā do . Wom.

n. m. (Slang). Impetus sive succussus in coitu. ṭalle navīsī k . v. n. lit . to register the number of strokes as above. (The phrase is derived from a witty story of a banyā , who with his son and daughter-in-law were frightened into submission to their wanton will by two wags who played off a practical joke on the Bania's notorious timidity). To do nothing; to dawdle; lounge; idle away; fiddle-faddle; fritter, or fool away the time; to fold one's arms; to waste one's time.
s. m. 1. Essential nature, bottom. 2. The sole of a shoe. 3. Protection.
(from طلا) s. m. The golden border of a turband.
wety: From Sanskrit.

ety: Prk. तलओ = S. तल+कः
def: s.m. Bottom, lowest part, base; depth; keel (of a boat, &c.); sole (of a shoe, &c.); a stand, support; protection (cf. tal)
subs: (Prk. tale; S. tale), adv. & postpn., At the bottom (of), at the foot (of, -ke, but this postpn. is very commonly omitted); under, underneath, beneath, below; down; on the ground

v.n. To come down, descend, dismount, &c.; to suffer loss; to come under, be under the authority or control (of, -ke); to fall under, be trodden upon, be crushed (by); to come under (anything, for shelter), to take shelter under
adv. One over or upon another, one after another, in succession; bottom upwards, upside-down, topsy turvy; confused
tale-band bāqī, s.f. A balance sheet
(-ke), To fall under, to be crushed (by); to lie under or beneath; to lie or sleep on the ground
Great confusion to arise, great commotion or convulsion to take place.
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: s.m. A stack, &c. = ṭāl, q.v. s.m. Pond, pool, tank, lake ( = tālāb).
more: s. m. 1. Evasion, &c. See ٿال. 2. A rick, heap, stack. ٿالا بالا بتانا ṭālā bālā batānā, To put off.

def: s.m. Putting off, postponement; evasion, &c. = ṭāl, q.v.
subs: (-ko), To make excuses; to evade, shuffle, &c. = ṭālam-ṭol karnā, q.v.s.v. ṭāl.

def: Talagang (a city in Talagang district, Punjab, Pakistan)
wety: Borrowed from Punjabi تلہ گنگ (tala gaṅg) / ਤਲਾਗੰਗ (talāgaṅg), named after the Hindus known as "Gangs" and due to it being below ground level, it was known as "tala".

def: s.m. The palmyra-tree or fan-palm ( = tāṛ, q.v.); — clapping the hands together; slapping the hands on the arms in defiance (in wrestling = tāṛī, q.v.); beating time (in music); musical time or measure; chime; a musical instrument of bell-metal or brass, a sort of cymbal (played with a stick); a pair of cymbals; — the glass or pebble of an eye-glass
subs: (˚la+ākh˚), s.f. A sort of vegetable perfume ( = tāl-parṇī)
s.m. The palm-leaf ( = tāṛ-patra, q.v.); — a hollow cylinder of gold (or a palm-leaf used as a substitute), with or without a ring attached, and thrust through the lobe of the ear as an ornament
s.f. A sort of anise, Anethum graveolens; a kind of vegetable perfume
s.m. A medicinal application to the eyes
v.n. To slap the arms defiantly (in wrestling = tāṛī ṭhoṅknā)
adj. & s.m. Having legs as long as a palm-tree; — name of a Rākshas; of a Daitya; of certain princes
v.t. To chime; to slap the arms ( = tāl ṭhoṅknā)
v.n. To get, or to be, out of tune
s.m. The medicinal herb Barleria (or Asteracantha) longifolia; the seeds of Solanum indicum (see makhānā)
s.m. Harmony, agreement, accord, &c. ( = ittifāq, q.v.); — tāl-mel khānā, v.n. To be in harmony, to agree, &c. ( = ittifāq honā)
tāl-vr̤int, s.m. A palm-leaf used as a fan; a fan.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ताल (tāla).

def: s.m. Surface, level surface, level; bottom, lowest part, &c. ( = talā, q.v.); lowness, inferiority of position; low ground; the place under, underneath part; sole of the foot; palm of the hand; ground, floor; deck (of a ship); back; — adv. & postpn., Under, beneath, &c. ( = tale); from underneath, from beneath
more: n. m. S. तल surface. Low grounds. tal k , v. a. 1. To put aside or apart. 2. To hide ( chhupānā ). 3. To steal ( churānā ). tal-nazrā , n. m. One who looks from under his eyes.
s. (dakh.) See تلا talā, Bottom; beneath, below, under.
subs: (tala+atala), s.m. The fourth of the seven divisions of the infernal regions
tal-ūpar, adv. One over or upon another (as a row of water-pots, &c.); up and down; upside down, bottom upwards, topsy-turvy, in confusion
adj. & adv. Turned upside down; trodden down, ruined, destroyed (particularly crops by the treading of cattle); lost, carried off, gone
v.t. To trample down, to ruin, &c.
tal-ćuʼā (from ćūnā), s.m. Light land above clay, land that quickly becomes soft and spongy in wet weather; menstruation
(from ćhuṭnā), s.f. Sediment, grounds, lees, dregs; refuse, leavings, scum, offals
(-ke), To put underneath, to put aside or apart, to hide (with the view of carrying off or stealing)
s.m. One who looks from under his eye-lids
wety: Inherited from Prakrit तिल (tila), from Sanskrit तिल (tila), borrowed from a Munda language.
